method: 2 parallel read-only agents (visual surface + functional gaps) + TWO personal raw-read verifications (file:line) + call-site analysis + attempt-to-disprove each claim
dedupedAgainst: plans/improve/report-2026-06-29.md (36 items R1-R7, F1-F8, U1-U12, P1-P6, V1-V2)

items.title: setAutostart OFF-path silently swallows reg-delete failure; UI shows off but registry entry remains
items.fix_approach: backend: replace `let _ = Command::new(reg).args([...delete...]).output()` with a status check returning Err on failure. frontend: wrap `await setAutostart(v)` in try/catch + rollback `autostart = !v` like sibling `toggleStartHidden` (SettingsTab.svelte:214-221).
items.verify: toggle autostart ON then corrupt reg permission (or feed an invalid enabled param via test harness); assert UI reverts to OFF and toast shows the error. Manual: enable, then disable -- confirm registry entry removed.
items.blast_radius: single caller: SettingsTab.svelte:368 <Toggle onCheckedChange={toggleAutostart}>. No other code path.

items.title: set_freellmapi_auth only WRITES, never deletes credentials from Credential Manager
items.fix_approach: add #[tauri::command] delete_freellmapi_auth(key: String) mirroring set_freellmapi_auth semantics with kr_delete. add `deleteAuth` IPC wrapper in src/lib/ipc.ts. add UI button next to hasEmail/hasToken badges (ProvidersTab.svelte:543-547) that asks confirm before calling.
items.verify: set credentials, confirm present in Credential Manager, click delete, confirm removed via Windows Credential Manager UI.
items.blast_radius: new IPC, no existing callers to break. New UI button in addition to existing login UI.

items.secondaryFile: src-tauri/Cargo.toml (plugin not in deps); src-tauri/capabilities/default.json; src-tauri/tauri.conf.json; .github/workflows/release.yml
items.title: No auto-update: tauri_plugin_updater not registered, no check/update commands, no UI entry point
items.fix_approach:
Per Tauri v2 docs (tauri-docs/plugin/updater.mdx):
  1. Install via `npm run tauri add updater` — idempotent, scaffolds Cargo feature + JS package @tauri-apps/plugin-updater + capability + tauri.conf section. Do NOT add manually.
  2. Generate signing keys: `npm run tauri signer generate -w ~/.tauri/castellyn.key` — store private key as env var (NOT .env, build won't read .env for updater).
  3. tauri.conf.json:
     - bundle.createUpdaterArtifacts: true
     - plugins.updater.pubkey: inline string of pubkey (NOT file path)
     - plugins.updater.endpoints: ['https://github.com/<user>/<repo>/releases/latest/download/latest.json']
  4. First release: use bundle.createUpdaterArtifacts: 'v1Compatible' until all users on v2 (Tauri v3 will remove this flag).
  5. latest.json shape: { version, notes, pub_date, platforms: { 'windows-x86_64': { signature, url }, ... } }
  6. CI: GitHub Actions with tauri-apps/tauri-action@v0 -- auto-uploads signed artifacts to GitHub Releases.
  7. Frontend IPC: check_for_update() (returns UpdaterResult), download_and_install() with restart-prompt UI in Settings → About, modal showing release notes before accept.
items.decision: Tauri Updater Plugin + full Settings UI (current best practice via `tauri add` scaffold)
items.verify: ship a v0.5.4 release with createUpdaterArtifacts=v1Compatible; install v0.5.3; click Check -- prompt with notes appears; accept; observe app reload to v0.5.4.
items.blast_radius: app-wide; affects existing users — pubkey rotation blocks them; .env vs env-var misconfig causes build artifacts to not sign.
items.currency_check: 2025-26 docs (Context7): `tauri add updater` is canonical, v1Compatible flag exists for migration, .env not loaded by updater artifact build
items.current_best_practice: tauri add updater + tauri-action@v0 GitHub Actions + inline pubkey string

items.title: WCAG light-theme contrast: 8 raw text-emerald-400/amber-400/red-400/500 usages fail 4.5:1
items.fix_approach: global swap: text-emerald-400 -> status-ok, text-amber-400 -> status-warn, text-red-400 -> status-bad, text-emerald-500 -> status-ok. .status-* classes already exist in app.css:273-298 with .light overrides; statusColor.ts:12-23 already exposes the helper.
items.verify: npm run build, switch to light theme, walk each affected tab. Tailwind purger should keep status-*. Manual: open light theme, scan analytics savings, schedule status, profiles viewer error, sync dirty, fork summary, profile usage badge.
items.blast_radius: 8 files; no consumers change (class names swap, visual equivalent in dark, improved in light).

items.title: Sortable table headers are mouse-only (no tabindex/role/onkeydown)
items.fix_approach: Per Svelte 5 + ARIA APG pattern: replace outer `` with ``. Native `
items.verify: Tab to a header, press Enter / Space, confirm sort toggles. Run npm test for DataTable-related tests (none currently exist for keyboard).
items.blast_radius: DataTable consumers (6 tabs: ForksTab, SyncTab, McpTab, ProfilesTab, ProvidersTab, PluginsTab) all get the fix free.

items.title: ToastHost focus-pause handlers dead: non-focusable div, onfocusin never fires for keyboard users
items.fix_approach: move onfocusin/onfocusout handlers from the host div to each individual toast .body (already focusable through the close button). Add tabindex=0 to .body so it can receive focus. Pause/resume via store actions from per-toast handlers.
items.verify: keyboard-only session: press Tab to focus a toast body or its close button, confirm pause kicks in for 1-2 sec after focus leaves.
items.blast_radius: ToastHost is global; affects all tabs.

items.title: ConfirmDialog missing aria-labelledby and aria-describedby; SR hears 'dialog' but not the title/message
items.fix_approach:
add ids in ConfirmDialog:

,

,
items.verify: open destructive confirm (e.g. Reinstall profiles with type-to-confirm), enable NVDA / VoiceOver, announce text expected.

items.title: Mixed icon system: Unicode glyphs (⑂⛁⧉☰⇅⟳) + color emoji (🔌🧩💲🔑📊🧭) coexist; two visual languages on one screen
items.fix_approach:
Per Lucide docs (lucide-icons/lucide/packages/svelte/README.md) -- canonical package for Svelte 5 is `@lucide/svelte` (NOT `lucide-svelte`, which is the deprecated Svelte 4 name).
Steps:
  1. `npm install @lucide/svelte`
  2. Per-icon imports (avoids the 'imports all icons, large bundle' warning):
     `import { Archive, Boxes, Library, Plug, Puzzle, Compass, RefreshCw, Copy, GitFork, FolderTree, ListPlus } from '@lucide/svelte'`
  3. Replace 8 places (ForksTab ⑂=GitFork, BackupTab ⛁=Archive, McpTab ⧉=Copy, ProfilesTab ☰=ListPlus, ProvidersTab 🔌=Plug & 🧩=Puzzle, EnvironmentsTab 🧭=Compass, StackHealthCard ⟳=RefreshCw, SecretInput ⧉=Copy) + Sidebar Unicode icons.
  4. Grep confirmed: Sidebar `items[].icon` referenced only in same file (line 25-38); blast radius local.
  5. Lucide sizes default to 24px, accept `size` and `stroke-width` props for color tokens.
items.decision: Lucide-style SVG via @lucide/svelte (current package, not deprecated 'lucide-svelte')
items.verify: open each tab visually, confirm icon style matches within and across.
items.blast_radius: 8 files; affects ProvidersTab:496, 662; StackHealthCard:111; etc.

items.title: ProfilesTab actions column 160px squished + duplicate provider cell controls (label-button + Edit-button)
items.fix_approach: widen actions column to 240px in DataTable columns definition (line 322); merge the duplicate provider controls into one (keep ghost `Изменить` only, make `providerLabel` span clickable alias).
items.verify: open Profiles tab, ensure all 3 actions visible without overlap; click provider cell, ensure still opens editor.
items.blast_radius: DataTable columns definition (1 line), provider cell snippet (lines 504-508). No behavior change.

items.title: ForksTab 5 KPIs: 2 clickable (button), 3 not (div) -- visually identical

items.verify: open Forks, click each KPI tile, confirm expectation matches.

Apply consistent spacing token (mb-sw-6 between sections).

items.fix_approach: add #[tauri::command] clone_repo(url, target) that spawns `git clone <url> <target>` with CREATE_NO_WINDOW; expose in ipc.ts; add `Clone here` button next to `Open` (ForksTab.svelte:304). Folder picker dialog for target path. Errors surface as toast.

items.title: 4 destructive actions in SessionsTab without askConfirm: deleteWorkspace, removeFav, deleteServer (SSH), TerminalPane relaunch
items.fix_approach: wrap each in `askConfirm(danger:true, ...)` before the mutation. Workspace: confirm 'This removes N panes of config'. Favorites: trivial confirm. SSH host: confirm with name in title. TerminalPane relaunch: confirm 'Loses scrollback'.

items.title: sendToAll confirm shows only command + count, no list of target panes (profiles/folders)
items.fix_approach: in sendToAll (line 246-256), build details array from `Object.entries(sessionIds).map(([key, id]) => \`${pane.tool}@${pane.profile} ${pane.cwd}\`)` and pass to ConfirmDialog.details (line 892).
items.verify: open 8 SSH panes, type `y` into Send All, confirm dialog shows pane list with profile/cwd/tool annotations.

items.title: MAX_PANES=12 (per-window FE) vs SESSION_LIMIT=24 (global BE) -- can exceed global via distribute-to-monitors
items.fix_approach: add #[tauri::command] global_session_count() reading state.0.lock().len(). Cache value in +page.svelte$state with debounce. SessionsTab.addPane gates on `MAX(globalCount + panes.length, MAX_PANES)`. Add hint in UI when nearing limit.
items.verify: spawn 22 panes across main + 2 monitors, try to add 3rd in main, confirm warning at 24.
items.blast_radius: SessionsTab addPane only; new IPC.

items.title: Bulk plugin ops sequential through single run lock (10 plugins x 2s = 20s downtime)
items.fix_approach: Promise.all(ids.map(id => runPlugin(action, id).then(...).catch(...))) with a per-line log buffer that flushes in spawn order. Each log line tagged with the plugin id so user can correlate. bulkActive flag still gates global run-done listeners (per +page.svelte:1241-1267).
items.verify: update 10 plugins; confirm log ordering shows all 10 in spawn order with id tags; ensure no other run is blocked during the sequence.
items.blast_radius: runBulkPlugins + onBulkPlugin callers; bulkActive flag.

items.title: Tray menu has only 3 items (show / check_all / quit)
items.fix_approach: Per Tauri v2 docs (tauri-docs/start/migrate/from-tauri-1.mdx, learn/system-tray.mdx): migrate existing tray_menu() (lib.rs:6166-6171) from deprecated `Menu::with_items(app, &[&...])` to current `MenuBuilder::new(app).text(id, label).separator().build()?` chain. Then extend with: Refresh forks, Start stack / Stop stack, Open Backup, Open Settings, Refresh providers, Cancel all (per F21). Each entry emits an IPC event handled by +page.svelte (mirrors tray-check-all pattern at line 1600-1612). Rebuild menu when language changes. Submenu pattern via SubmenuBuilder if user wants grouping (e.g. 'Stack > Start / Stop' under one parent).
items.decision: Расширенный набор (Refresh forks, Start/Stop stack, Open Backup, Open Settings, Refresh providers, Cancel all)
items.verify: right-click tray, see new entries, click each, confirm action runs.
items.blast_radius: +page.svelte adds 5+ IPC listeners mirroring tray-check-all pattern (line 1600-1612).

items.title: Tray 'Quit' = app.exit(0) without confirm, kills PTY sessions
items.fix_approach: in on_menu_event, replace `app.exit(0)` with emit('tray-quit-request'); +page.svelte shows confirm dialog 'N sessions running, quit anyway?'; only after confirm call app.exit().
items.verify: open 3 sessions, click Quit, confirm dialog appears with session count.
items.blast_radius: +page.svelte adds new listener for tray-quit-request.

items.title: Tray 'Check all' force-jumps to Updates tab, stealing focus mid-edit
items.fix_approach: only switch tab if `active !== 'updates'` AND no input is currently focused. If user is in a field, run in background and toast 'Check started'.
items.verify: open Settings (focus a text input), click tray Check All, confirm tab does NOT jump and run starts.
items.blast_radius: tray-check-all handler only.

items.title: No global 'Cancel all' -- cancel_run only kills RunState slot, leaves per-repo forks + PTY alive
items.fix_approach: add cancel_all RPC in lib.rs: kill RunState slot + iterate ForkRuns map + session_kill on every PTY id. Emit 'cancel-all-done' event. +page.svelte: (1) Ctrl+Shift+Escape binding via onGlobalKey (line 1398-1427); (2) Tray menu 'Cancel all' entry per F18.
items.decision: И в tray menu (Ctrl+Shift+Esc + tray entry)
items.verify: start fork check + a long session, press Ctrl+Shift+Esc OR click tray Cancel all, confirm both die.
items.blast_radius: +page.svelte + Console.svelte (add button) + onGlobalKey (line 1398-1427).

items.title: restoreLayout toast offers 'N panes on M monitors' without listing contents (tool/profile/cwd)
items.fix_approach: build summary string from localStorage MLKEY contents (tool@profile cwd, joined by '; '); embed in toast `detail` field so user sees 'Claude@cc1 ~/proj1; Claude@cc2 ~/proj2; ...' before clicking restore.
items.verify: save a layout with 3 panes, restart, see toast detail shows the 3 pane specs.
items.blast_radius: SessionsTab layout save/restore flow only.

items.title: HomeTab cards are read-only -- no quick actions (Run all / Start stack); no Stack/Sessions chip
items.fix_approach: (a) quick-action row under overall badge with [Run check all] [Refresh forks] [Start stack] [Stop stack] + per-chip action button (e.g. on Drift chip -> Relink, on Profiles chip -> Repair All). (b) Add 2 new chips: stack-running-badge + sessions-active-count, fed from stackData and from SessionsTab via shared store. (c) Wire each chip's onAction handler at +page.svelte.
items.verify: open Home, click Run check all (Updates opens with check), click Drift chip action (Relink runs), click Stack chip (state visible).
items.blast_radius: HomeTab + onRefresh prop already passes reloadHome; need to wire startRun('all','check') and stack-session signals.

items.fix_approach: add #[tauri::command] canonical_skills_dir() returning the canonical root (per skill_sets source priority). Mirror gateway_base_url pattern. +page.svelte:1283 caches the result and uses it instead of skillsData[0].
